title: Fugazzeta Pizza Served at a Traditional Pizzeria on Avenida Corrientes in Buenos Aires City
description: Buenos Aires, Ciudad Autónoma de Buenos Aires: A triangular slice of fugazzeta, the iconic onion-topped Argentine pizza, rests on a simple white plate under the warm, ambient glow of a historic pizzeria. The close-up composition captures the glistening, tender translucent onions layered generously over a molten bed of melted mozzarella and herbs, emphasizing the soft, airy texture of the thick, doughy crust. Light catches the slightly charred edges of the crust and the crisp, browned tips of the scallions, highlighting the culinary dedication found along the bustling Avenida Corrientes. The shallow depth of field isolates the slice, drawing the viewer into the rich, savory sensory experience of a classic Buenos Aires meal. This modest plate reflects the long-standing tradition of pizza culture in the capital, where simple ingredients are elevated by generations of artisanal practice. As the steam gently rises, the image evokes the nostalgic atmosphere of late-night dining in a city that truly never sleeps. Documenting this specific style of pizza is essential to understanding the Italian-Argentine syncretic heritage that defines the region’s unique gastronomical identity. The photograph serves as a testament to the enduring simplicity of local comfort food amidst the urban intensity of Corrientes. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.

json_ld.name: Fugazzeta Pizza Served at a Traditional Pizzeria on Avenida Corrientes in Buenos Aires City
json_ld.description: A close-up shot of an authentic fugazzeta pizza slice on a plate in Buenos Aires, highlighting the traditional onion and cheese topping.
